text: OSINT Defender tracking: Iran fired its 8th missile salvo of Day 23 targeting Arad, Dimona, Eilat, Beersheba, and Kiryat Gat. Cumulative Day 23 injuries estimated 150-180; 11 in serious condition. Air defenses performed better in later salvos. IDF confirmed no Day 23 fatalities from missile fire.
verificationNote: Consistent with Times of Israel liveblog and Al Jazeera Day 23 coverage.

text: War Monitor: Hezbollah rocket killed 60-year-old Israeli civilian Ofer Moskovitz at Misgav Am. IDF investigating potential friendly fire — no sirens before strike. Defense Minister Katz ordered immediate destruction of all Litani River bridges. Lebanon confirmed death toll now 1,021+. IDF ground operations in southern Lebanon continuing 7th day.
verificationNote: Death confirmed by Israeli emergency services. Katz bridge order confirmed by Times of Israel.

text: Intel Crab: Iran FM Araghchi confirmed Japan granted Hormuz transit rights joining China, India, Pakistan. IRGC simultaneously building formal vetting/registration system for approved vessels — ships must pre-submit ownership, cargo destination to IRGC command. One tanker reportedly paid $2M for passage. 40,000+ seafarers stranded on both sides of strait.
verificationNote: Araghchi statement confirmed by Japan Times and Al Jazeera. Vetting system confirmed by Al Jazeera March 20 report.

text: Sentinel Defense: Sen. Murkowski (R-AK) signaling push for congressional war authorization vote if Trump orders ground troops to Iran. Dem senators blocked SECOND War Powers vote (March 18) by Republicans. Trump administration still operating without formal war authorization for Operation Epic Fury entering Week 4. Legal scholars debate whether AUMF 2001 covers current operations.
verificationNote: Murkowski quote confirmed by CNN. War Powers vote confirmed by Sen. Booker's office statement.

text: MENA OSINT: EU sanctions 19 Iranian officials (Kallas March 22). Russia called Natanz strike 'a blatant violation of international law' — Putin pledged Moscow remains 'loyal friend to Iran.' China called for immediate ceasefire and condemned military operations targeting civilian infrastructure. 22-nation joint statement (incl. UK, France, Germany, Japan) condemned Iranian vessel attacks and offered to help secure Hormuz.
verificationNote: EU sanctions confirmed by Kallas X post. Russia statement confirmed by CBS liveblog. 22-nation statement confirmed by UK gov website.

text: Intel Crab: CENTCOM Admiral Cooper confirmed US dropped GBU-72 Advanced Penetrator 5,000-lb bunker busters on hardened Iranian anti-ship missile bunkers near Strait of Hormuz — these are the largest conventional penetrating bombs in the US arsenal, designed for deeply buried facilities. 8,000+ Iranian targets struck total, 130 naval vessels destroyed. Cooper: 'will not stop.'
verificationNote: CENTCOM press statement confirmed by Gulf News and Maritime Executive.

text: OSINT Defender: Netanyahu at Arad strike site (Day 23): 'What more proof do you need that this regime that threatens the entire world has to be stopped? Israel and the United States are working together for the entire world.' Called on international leaders to formally join the coalition. Ben Gurion flight restrictions effective Monday. IDF chief Zamir says Hezbollah fight 'only just begun' — weeks more of fighting expected.
verificationNote: Netanyahu quote confirmed by Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel liveblog March 22.

text: War Monitor: CRITICAL — Iranian cluster munition ballistic missiles scattered submunitions across 7+ central Israel sites including Tel Aviv. 15 wounded. Cluster warhead fragments confirmed by IDF. This is internationally condemned munitions use against civilian-populated areas. Alma Research Center tracking: 29 Iranian attack waves weekend March 20-21 alone — 41% targeting southern Israel, 31% Tel Aviv area. Iran missile degradation now 90%+ but ongoing.
verificationNote: IDF confirmed cluster fragments. Alma Research Center daily report March 22.

text: Araghchi Watch: Iranian FM Araghchi posted on X: 'Strait of Hormuz is not closed. Ships hesitate because insurers fear the war of choice you initiated — not Iran. No insurer — and no Iranian — will be swayed by more threats. Try respect. Freedom of Navigation cannot exist without Freedom of Trade.' Trump 48-hr ultimatum expires Monday 7:44 PM ET — Iran has shown zero compliance signal.
verificationNote: Araghchi X post confirmed. Trump ultimatum via Truth Social confirmed by NPR, CBS, CNN.

text: MENA OSINT: Iran President Pezeshkian on X: 'The illusion of erasing Iran from the map shows desperation against the will of a history-making nation. Threats and terror only strengthen our unity. The Strait of Hormuz is open to all except those who violate our soil.' Called on BRICS to 'play an independent role in halting aggressions.' Zero sign of Iran complying with Trump's 48-hr power plant ultimatum.
verificationNote: Pezeshkian X post confirmed by Tribune India and Al Jazeera Day 23 explainer.

text: ISW Research: US temporarily eased sanctions on ~140 million barrels of Iranian oil at sea (March 20) — allowing allies to purchase to contain energy prices. Critics call it 'funding a war against itself.' AAA gas now $3.942/gal national average (+23% from pre-war $3.21). Brent crude trading ~$112 intraday on Trump ultimatum risk premium (+40% from pre-war). European TTF gas €59/MWh (+39% YoY).
verificationNote: US sanctions easing confirmed by NBC News March 20. Oil/gas prices confirmed by AAA, OilpriceAPI, Investing.com.

text: ACLED documented nearly 2,300 distinct conflict events across 29 of Iran's 31 provinces as of Day 23. Tehran endured the heaviest bombardment; Hormuz province and coastal areas also heavily hit. Unprecedented strike density for a 3-week air campaign.
verificationNote: ACLED database publicly accessible. Cited by Al Jazeera Day 23 analysis.

displayName: Institute for the Study of War
text: IDF overnight strikes on Tehran targeted missile production capacity at IRGC central production compounds and fuel facilities. Power substation strikes in western Tehran and Karaj suggest broader infrastructure targeting campaign is expanding beyond weapons sites.
verificationNote: Based on confirmed IDF operational announcements and Iran International reporting on Tehran strikes.

text: Asian markets fell sharply on Trump's Hormuz ultimatum expiry risk: Nikkei 225 -3.5%, KOSPI -4.9%, Hang Seng -2.7%. Brent crude hit $114.09/bbl (+1.69%); WTI $100.29 (+2%). Markets pricing in power plant strikes and complete Hormuz closure by Monday.
verificationNote: Market data confirmed by CBS News liveblog reporting on Asian market opens March 22-23.

text: NATO Sec-Gen Rutte: 22 nations joining coalition to ensure freedom of navigation through Strait of Hormuz 'as soon as possible.' France's Operation Aspides maritime escort mission with ~12 ships is setting up. Germany and Greece explicitly ruled out military participation.
verificationNote: Rutte statement confirmed by Times of Israel liveblog. France/Germany details from CBS News and Bloomberg reporting.

text: IRGC-affiliated Tasnim released a target list naming US tech company offices in Israel and Gulf states — Google, Microsoft, Palantir, IBM, Nvidia, Oracle — as legitimate targets in any future 'infrastructure war.' Signals Iran preparing asymmetric cyber-physical retaliation options if power plants struck.
verificationNote: Reported by Al Jazeera liveblog March 22 and CBS News. Tasnim is IRGC-affiliated but claims should be treated as threat signaling.

text: Only 21 tankers total have transited Hormuz since Feb 28, vs. 100+/day pre-war. AIS data shows 13 vessels/day average since March 1 (down from 153/day pre-conflict). 400+ vessels still anchored at anchor. Saudi Arabia's East-West Pipeline bypass (Yanbu Red Sea port) now operational as alternative route.
verificationNote: AIS vessel tracking data confirmed by CNBC Hormuz tracker (March 18). Saudi pipeline details from Bloomberg March 22.

text: IDF overnight strikes confirmed on 4 IRGC ballistic missile production and fuel facilities in Tehran and Karaj. Power substation hits causing localized blackouts in western Tehran reported across multiple Iranian Telegram channels. Air defense suppression operations also confirmed in the capital.
verificationNote: Multiple corroborating Iranian social media reports. Confirmed by Iran International.

text: Hezbollah rocket strike confirmed in Nahariya (Day 24 morning). 6 wounded per Israeli emergency services. IDF 36th Division launched new targeted operation expanding forward defense area in south Lebanon toward Litani. Approximately 400+ Hezbollah fighters killed since Lebanon ground ops began.
verificationNote: IDF and Israeli emergency services confirmed Nahariya strike and casualties.

text: Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum expires tonight (~7:44 PM ET). Iran has explicitly rejected it. IRGC doctrine shift to offensive posture now includes Gulf power plants, desalination, and US base energy infrastructure as targets. Analysts tracking whether US executes power plant strikes or issues second ultimatum.
verificationNote: Ultimatum timing confirmed by Axios. IRGC counter-threat confirmed by PBS, NBC, PBS NewsHour.

text: IDF killed Abu Khalil Barji, Radwan Force special ops commander, in Majdal Selm, southern Lebanon via drone strike. Barji was directing cross-border attacks and coordinating Hezbollah-IRGC liaison for the Lebanon front. One of the highest-value Hezbollah targets eliminated since war began.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel, attributed to IDF Spokesperson.

text: Geolocated strike footage from Lebanon confirms Qasmiyeh Bridge on the coastal road and Zrarieh Bridge both destroyed. Two additional Litani crossings under watch. Lebanese President Aoun calling bridge demolitions a prelude to a ground invasion north of the river.
verificationNote: CGTN footage of Qasmiyeh Bridge strike and Al Jazeera reporting confirmed. Zrarieh confirmed by Times of Israel.

text: Turkey FM Fidan conducted active shuttle diplomacy on March 22 — calls with Araghchi (Iran), Egyptian FM, Kallas (EU), and US officials. Turkey positioning as key mediator but Fidan notes a “growing assessment that US and Israel initial positions are drifting apart” which risks prolonging the war.
verificationNote: Turkey FM Fidan statements confirmed by Israel Hayom and TRT World.

text: Trump posted on Truth Social claiming "productive" US-Iran talks and announced a 5-day pause on strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure. Key detail: Iran's FM Araghchi immediately denied any contact, calling the claim an attempt to mislead oil traders. Iran's Fars News cited a government source confirming "no direct or indirect contact" with the US.
verificationNote: Trump post confirmed across all major outlets. Iran denial confirmed by Araghchi statement and Fars News source.

text: IDF confirmed a "wide-scale wave of strikes" on Tehran in early morning March 23 — targeting IRGC police HQ, Basij militia base, rocket engine production facility, and Isfahan Internal Security Force HQ. Al Jazeera's Tehran correspondent called the explosions "unprecedented" in scale, especially on the city's eastern side. Separate CENTCOM strike destroyed the Qom Turbine Engine Production Plant used for IRGC drone manufacturing.
verificationNote: IDF formally announced the operation. CENTCOM confirmed Qom plant with satellite imagery.

text: Day 24 civilian casualty summary from Iranian cities: Tabriz — 6 killed, 6 injured (residential buildings). Khorramabad — 1 child killed, body pulled from rubble. Urmia — buildings "flattened," rescue teams searching. Bandar Abbas — 1 IRIB guard killed at state radio transmitter. Total new confirmed civilian deaths in this morning's strikes: at least 8.
verificationNote: Tabriz confirmed by crisis management official Farshi. Khorramabad by Fars News. Bandar Abbas by Anadolu and Ynet.

text: Iran Defense Council issued an escalatory threat to mine the Strait of Hormuz and the entire Persian Gulf if US or Israeli forces attack Iran's coastline or islands. This would be a qualitative escalation beyond the existing effective blockade — mining represents a persistent threat to all shipping, not just selective denial. IRGC also specifically threatened Gulf power plants: "If you hit electricity, we hit electricity."
verificationNote: Iran Defense Council statement confirmed by Al Jazeera and Times of Israel liveblogs.

text: USS Tripoli (LHA-7) with 2,000+ Marines reportedly moving to the region as the US prepares a "weeks-long operation" to force open the Strait of Hormuz — potentially including seizure of Kharg Island (Iran's main oil export terminal). This plan was in motion before Trump's 5-day pause announcement. The pause buys time but the military option is being readied.
verificationNote: USS Tripoli movement confirmed by Times of Israel. Kharg Island plan reported by Israeli officials.

text: Israel blew up the Qasmiyeh coastal highway bridge over the Litani River — the fifth Litani-area bridge struck since March 2. Lebanon President Aoun called it a "dangerous escalation" and "prelude to a ground invasion" north of the Litani. Defense Minister Katz ordered ALL Litani River bridges demolished. Around 150,000 civilians in southern Lebanon face restricted movement.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, and CGTN footage of bridge strike.
